title: NTC Temperature Sensor Short Circuit
shortDescription: The NTC temperature sensor has a short circuit.
fullDescription: Error F01 on Miele dryers indicates the NTC temperature sensor has shorted internally. The dryer cannot monitor exhaust or drum air temperature and will stop to prevent overheating.

causes.cause: Sensor wiring is damaged or pinched

fixSteps.title: Reset the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 5 minutes, then restart. If F01 returns, proceed with diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Access the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the dryer's top or rear panel. Locate the NTC sensor in the exhaust duct or near the heating element.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature expect 10-15 kOhms. Near-zero reading confirms short circuit.

fixSteps.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the faulty sensor from its housing and install the replacement. Reconnect wiring and reassemble.

whenToCallPro: If sensor and wiring test fine, the control board input may be faulty. Contact a Miele technician.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dryer F01 NTC temperature sensor error fix

faq.q: What does the NTC sensor do in a dryer?
faq.a: The NTC sensor monitors air temperature inside the dryer drum or exhaust duct. It helps the control board regulate heating to protect clothes and prevent overheating.

faq.q: Can I use the dryer with F01?
faq.a: No. The dryer will not heat or may not run at all with F01 active. The sensor must be replaced for safe operation.

faq.q: How much does a Miele dryer NTC sensor cost?
faq.a: Genuine Miele NTC sensors for dryers cost between 15 and 35 dollars. The repair is straightforward for someone with basic skills.

title: NTC Temperature Sensor Open Circuit
shortDescription: The NTC temperature sensor circuit is open.
fullDescription: Error F02 means the control board reads infinite resistance from the NTC sensor, indicating an open circuit in the sensor or its wiring. The dryer cannot monitor temperature.

causes.cause: NTC sensor has failed open circuit
Lint buildup around connector causing poor contact

symptoms: Dryer runs but clothes remain damp

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes. A vibration-loosened connector may reseat.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connections
fixSteps.instruction: Access the NTC sensor and push the connector firmly until it clicks.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and measure with multimeter. Expect 10-15 kOhms at room temperature. OL reading confirms open circuit.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Swap the faulty sensor for a new one. Ensure secure connection.

whenToCallPro: If sensor and wiring are intact, the control board may have a damaged input.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dryer F02 NTC sensor open circuit error fix

faq.q: Difference between F01 and F02?
faq.a: F01 is a short circuit (zero resistance) while F02 is an open circuit (infinite resistance). Both indicate NTC sensor failure.

faq.q: Can a loose connector cause F02?
faq.a: Yes. Lint and vibration can loosen connectors over time. Always check connections first.

faq.q: Is F02 common on Miele dryers?
faq.a: F02 is relatively common, especially in older dryers. The NTC sensor is an affordable and easy replacement.

title: Heating Element Fault
shortDescription: The heating element or its control circuit has failed.
fullDescription: Error F03 indicates a problem with the dryer's heating element or the relay that controls it. The element may be open circuit, or the relay may be stuck.

causes.cause: Heating relay on control board is faulty
Wiring to heating element is damaged

symptoms: Dryer runs but clothes stay damp

fixSteps.title: Check thermal fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the thermal fuse on the heating element housing. Test with multimeter for continuity. A blown fuse shows open circuit.

fixSteps.title: Test heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the element and measure resistance. Expect 15-30 ohms. Open circuit (OL) means the element is burned out.

fixSteps.title: Check element wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect all wires from the element to the control board for damage or loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Replace element or fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the faulty component. The thermal fuse is a simple clip-on replacement. The element requires removing its housing.

whenToCallPro: If the element and fuse are fine, the control board heating relay may be faulty. Professional diagnosis is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dryer F03 heating element error fix

faq.q: Why did my dryer thermal fuse blow?
faq.a: Thermal fuses blow when the dryer overheats, often due to a blocked lint filter, restricted exhaust vent, or failed thermostat. Always check ventilation before replacing the fuse.

faq.q: Can I bypass the thermal fuse?
faq.a: Never bypass a thermal fuse. It is a critical safety device that prevents fires. Always replace with the correct rated fuse.

faq.q: How long does a Miele dryer heating element last?
faq.a: Typically 8-15 years with normal use. Keeping the lint filter and exhaust vent clean helps extend its life.

title: Overtemperature Protection
shortDescription: The dryer temperature has exceeded the safe maximum limit.
fullDescription: Error F04 is a safety shutdown indicating the dryer air temperature exceeded the maximum safe threshold. This protects clothes and the machine from heat damage.

causes.cause: Exhaust vent is clogged or crushed
Thermostat has failed allowing continuous heating

symptoms: Very hot air or burning smell

fixSteps.title: Clean the lint filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and thoroughly clean the lint filter. Wash it with warm water and a brush to remove any waxy residue from dryer sheets.

fixSteps.title: Check exhaust vent
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the exhaust hose from the back of the dryer. Check for lint buildup, kinks, or crushed sections. Clean the entire vent run to the exterior.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermostat
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the cycling thermostat and high-limit thermostat. Test both for continuity. A failed thermostat allows uncontrolled heating.

fixSteps.title: Let dryer cool and test
fixSteps.instruction: Allow the dryer to cool for 30 minutes. Clean all lint paths and restart. If F04 returns with clean vents, the thermostat or NTC sensor needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If F04 occurs with clean vents and working thermostats, the heating system needs professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dryer F04 overheating overtemperature error fix

faq.q: Is F04 a fire risk?
faq.a: F04 indicates overheating which is a fire risk. Always clean lint filters before every load and have the exhaust vent professionally cleaned annually.

faq.q: How do I clean my dryer exhaust vent?
faq.a: Disconnect the vent hose, use a vent cleaning brush to clear lint from the duct, and check the exterior vent cap for blockages.

faq.q: Can dryer sheets cause overheating?
faq.a: Dryer sheet residue can coat the lint filter, reducing airflow even when the filter appears clean. Wash the filter with warm soapy water monthly.

title: Drive Motor Fault
shortDescription: The drive motor is not running or not reaching the correct speed.
fullDescription: Error F05 indicates the drum drive motor has a fault. The motor is not starting, not reaching target speed, or drawing excessive current.

causes.cause: Drive belt is broken or slipping

fixSteps.title: Check the drive belt
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top panel and check the drive belt. A broken or slipped belt prevents drum rotation.

fixSteps.title: Test motor rotation
fixSteps.instruction: With belt removed, try to spin the drum by hand. It should turn freely. If stiff, the drum bearings may be seized.

fixSteps.title: Test motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect motor wires and measure winding resistance (typically 2-10 ohms). Check for ground faults.

fixSteps.title: Replace belt or motor
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the broken belt by routing it around the drum and motor pulley. If the motor is faulty, replace the motor assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the motor tests faulty, professional replacement ensures proper belt tension and motor alignment.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dryer F05 drive motor error fix

faq.q: How do I check if the belt is broken?
faq.a: Remove the top panel and visually inspect the belt running between the drum and motor pulley. A broken belt will be hanging loose.

faq.q: Can I replace the Miele dryer belt myself?
faq.a: Yes, belt replacement is a common DIY repair. Route the new belt around the drum, then loop it around the tensioner and motor pulley.

faq.q: How long does a Miele dryer belt last?
faq.a: Typically 8-12 years with normal use. Regular cleaning and not overloading the dryer helps extend belt life.

title: Drum Speed Sensor Error
shortDescription: The drum speed sensor is not detecting proper drum rotation.
fullDescription: Error F06 means the drum speed sensor (tachometer) is not receiving a valid signal. The dryer cannot verify the drum is rotating at the correct speed.

causes.cause: Drum not rotating due to belt issue

fixSteps.title: Check belt
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the drive belt is intact and properly tensioned.

fixSteps.title: Inspect speed sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the speed sensor on or near the motor. Check that it is securely mounted.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Measure sensor resistance with a multimeter. Compare to specifications (typically 50-200 ohms).

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Swap the faulty sensor and ensure proper alignment.

whenToCallPro: If sensor and belt are fine, the motor or control board may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dryer F06 drum speed sensor error fix

faq.q: What is a drum speed sensor?
faq.a: A small sensor that detects how fast the drum is spinning. The control board uses this to maintain proper drying speed and detect problems.

faq.q: Can a broken belt cause F06?
faq.a: Yes. If the belt is broken, the drum won't spin, and the speed sensor reports no rotation, triggering F06.

faq.q: Is F06 common?
faq.a: F06 is moderately common, often caused by belt issues rather than the sensor itself. Always check the belt first.

title: Motor Blocked
shortDescription: The drum motor is blocked or severely overloaded.
fullDescription: Error F07 means the motor cannot rotate the drum due to a mechanical blockage, seized bearings, or a foreign object.

causes.cause: Foreign object between drum and housing

fixSteps.title: Reduce load
fixSteps.instruction: Remove some items from the drum. An overloaded drum can strain the motor.

fixSteps.title: Check for foreign objects
fixSteps.instruction: Rotate the drum by hand and listen for scraping. Check between the drum and housing.

fixSteps.title: Check drum bearings
fixSteps.instruction: Spin the drum by hand. It should rotate smoothly. Rough rotation indicates worn bearings.

fixSteps.title: Inspect support rollers
fixSteps.instruction: Access the drum support rollers and check for wear or seizure. Replace if they do not spin freely.

whenToCallPro: Drum bearing replacement is a major repair best handled by a professional.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dryer F07 drum blocked motor error fix

faq.q: Why is my dryer drum stuck?
faq.a: Most commonly due to a foreign object (coin, button, etc.) or worn drum support rollers. Items can fall between the drum edge and housing.

faq.q: Can I replace drum bearings myself?
faq.a: Drum bearing replacement on Miele dryers is a major disassembly. It is doable but takes 2-4 hours and requires specific tools.

faq.q: How do I check the support rollers?
faq.a: Remove the front or rear panel to access the rollers at the bottom of the drum. They should spin freely by hand.

title: Condenser Fan Error
shortDescription: The condenser fan is not running at the correct speed (heat pump models).
fullDescription: Error F08 on Miele heat pump dryers indicates the condenser fan is not operating properly. This fan is essential for the heat exchange process in heat pump models.

causes.cause: Fan blade is blocked by lint
Control board not driving the fan

symptoms: Dryer takes very long to dry

fixSteps.title: Clean the condenser
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the condenser unit (usually accessible from the front) and rinse it under running water. Clear all lint buildup.

fixSteps.title: Check fan for blockages
fixSteps.instruction: Access the fan area and remove any lint or debris blocking the fan blades.

fixSteps.title: Test fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the fan motor and test winding resistance. Also check that the fan spins freely by hand.

fixSteps.title: Replace fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove mounting screws, disconnect wiring, and install replacement fan motor.

whenToCallPro: If the fan and motor are fine but F08 persists, the control board may not be sending the signal. Call a technician.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dryer F08 condenser fan error heat pump fix

faq.q: What is a condenser fan in a dryer?
faq.a: In heat pump dryers, the condenser fan helps cool the refrigerant after it absorbs moisture from clothes. Without it, the heat pump cannot function efficiently.

faq.q: How often should I clean the condenser?
faq.a: Miele recommends cleaning the condenser unit every few months or when the indicator shows. Regular cleaning prevents F08.

faq.q: Can lint cause F08?
faq.a: Yes. Lint buildup around the fan can block it or slow it down. Always clean the lint filter before every load and the condenser regularly.

title: Communication Error
shortDescription: Internal communication between control modules has failed.
fullDescription: Error F09 signals a communication failure between the main control board and another module inside the dryer.

causes.cause: Ribbon cable or connector is loose

fixSteps.title: Hard reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 15 minutes to fully discharge all capacitors.

fixSteps.title: Reseat connections
fixSteps.instruction: Open panels and reseat all ribbon cables and connectors between boards.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect control board area for moisture or condensation. Dry and ensure housing seals are intact.

fixSteps.title: Professional diagnosis
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, a technician with Miele diagnostic tools can identify the failed module.

whenToCallPro: F09 is best diagnosed with Miele's proprietary tools. Contact a certified technician.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dryer F09 communication error fix

faq.q: Can humidity cause F09?
faq.a: Yes. Dryers in humid laundry rooms can develop condensation on control boards, causing communication errors.

faq.q: Does F09 always mean the board is broken?
faq.a: No. Often it is just a loose cable. Always try reseating connections before replacing boards.

faq.q: How do I prevent F09?
faq.a: Ensure good ventilation in your laundry room and use a surge protector to protect the dryer's electronics.

title: Lint Filter Sensor Error
shortDescription: The lint filter sensor is not detecting the filter correctly.
fullDescription: Error F10 indicates the sensor that checks lint filter presence or cleanliness is malfunctioning. The dryer may refuse to start if it cannot confirm the filter is in place.

causes.cause: Lint filter is not properly seated

fixSteps.title: Reseat the lint filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lint filter, clean it thoroughly, and reinsert it firmly until it clicks into place.

fixSteps.title: Clean the sensor area
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the lint filter slot and the sensor contacts. Lint buildup can prevent the sensor from detecting the filter.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Access the sensor behind the filter slot. Test it with a multimeter for proper operation when the filter is inserted and removed.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor does not respond correctly, replace it.

whenToCallPro: If sensor replacement does not fix F10, check the control board input.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dryer F10 lint filter sensor error fix

faq.q: Why does my dryer say the lint filter is missing?
faq.a: The filter may not be fully inserted, or the sensor that detects it is dirty or faulty. Clean the filter and sensor area first.

faq.q: How often should I clean the lint filter?
faq.a: Before every single load. A clean lint filter improves drying performance and prevents overheating.

faq.q: Can a dirty filter sensor cause problems?
faq.a: Yes. Lint and residue on the sensor contacts prevent proper detection. Clean the sensor area when you clean the filter.

title: Drain Pump Error
shortDescription: The condensate drain pump is not working (condenser/heat pump models).
fullDescription: Error F11 on condenser and heat pump dryers means the condensate drain pump cannot empty the water collection container or pump water out through the drain hose.

causes.cause: Water collection container is not properly seated
Drain pump is clogged with lint

fixSteps.title: Check water container
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the water collection container, empty it, and reinsert it firmly. Ensure it clicks into place.

fixSteps.title: Clean the pump area
fixSteps.instruction: Access the drain pump area and clean any lint or debris. The pump inlet can become clogged.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: If the dryer is plumbed to a drain, check the hose for kinks or blockages.

fixSteps.title: Test pump motor
fixSteps.instruction: Listen for the pump running. If silent, test motor resistance with a multimeter.

whenToCallPro: If the pump motor is faulty, replacement is relatively straightforward but a technician ensures no leaks.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dryer F11 condensate drain pump error fix

faq.q: Why does my condenser dryer stop with the container full?
faq.a: The drain pump may be clogged or failed. Empty the container, clean the pump area, and check if the pump runs during the next cycle.

faq.q: Can I plumb my Miele dryer directly to a drain?
faq.a: Yes. Most Miele condenser and heat pump dryers support direct drain connection. Use the supplied hose and follow installation guidelines.

faq.q: How often should I empty the water container?
faq.a: After every load if not plumbed to a drain. The dryer will display a reminder when the container is full.

title: Water Level Sensor Error
shortDescription: The condensate water level sensor is faulty (condenser/heat pump models).
fullDescription: Error F12 indicates the sensor that monitors the condensate water collection container level is not working correctly.

causes.cause: Container not seated properly against sensor

fixSteps.title: Reseat the container
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and reinsert the water container, ensuring it is fully and properly seated.

fixSteps.title: Clean sensor contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the sensor contacts on both the container and the machine with a damp cloth.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Access the water level sensor and test it with a multimeter. It should change state when the container is full vs empty.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If faulty, replace the water level sensor unit.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor and container are fine but F12 persists, the control board may need testing.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dryer F12 water level container sensor error

faq.q: Why does my dryer think the container is full when it is empty?
faq.a: Dirty sensor contacts or a misaligned container are the most common causes. Clean and reseat the container.

faq.q: Can I bypass the water level sensor?
faq.a: No. The sensor prevents overflow. If you plumb the dryer to a drain, the container sensor still monitors for proper drainage.

faq.q: Does the sensor need maintenance?
faq.a: Clean the sensor contacts whenever you empty the water container to prevent buildup from affecting readings.

title: Motor Inverter Error
shortDescription: The motor inverter or frequency converter has detected a fault.
fullDescription: Error F13 indicates the motor inverter module has detected overcurrent, undervoltage, or overtemperature. The inverter controls the brushless drum motor.

fixSteps.title: Cool down
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 30 minutes to allow the inverter to cool.

fixSteps.title: Check ventilation
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the dryer has adequate airflow around it for inverter cooling.

fixSteps.title: Test motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect motor and test winding resistance for shorts or opens.

fixSteps.title: Replace inverter or motor
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the faulty component based on testing results.

whenToCallPro: Motor and inverter diagnosis requires professional expertise to avoid replacing the wrong expensive component.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dryer F13 motor inverter error fix

faq.q: What causes the inverter to overheat?
faq.a: Poor ventilation around the dryer, continuous use without breaks, or an overloaded drum can cause inverter overheating.

faq.q: Is inverter replacement expensive?
faq.a: Yes. The inverter module costs 180-380 dollars. Combined with labor, this is one of the more expensive dryer repairs.

faq.q: Can I prevent F13?
faq.a: Ensure good ventilation, don't overload the drum, and allow the dryer to cool between heavy loads.

title: Door Lock Error
shortDescription: The door lock mechanism is not engaging or releasing correctly.
fullDescription: Error F14 means the dryer door lock is not confirming proper closure. The dryer requires a locked door before starting any cycle.

causes.cause: Door lock mechanism has worn out

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes. The door should unlock when power is removed.

fixSteps.title: Check alignment
fixSteps.instruction: Close the door firmly and check that the catch properly engages the lock.

fixSteps.title: Test door lock
fixSteps.instruction: Access the lock mechanism and test with a multimeter for proper operation.

fixSteps.title: Replace lock
fixSteps.instruction: Remove screws, disconnect wiring, install new lock assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the lock mechanism is fine but F14 persists, the control board may not be sending the lock signal.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dryer F14 door lock error fix

faq.q: How do I unlock a stuck Miele dryer door?
faq.a: Unplug the dryer for 5-10 minutes. Most Miele models release the door lock when power is removed.

faq.q: Can I start the dryer without the door lock?
faq.a: No. The door lock is a safety feature that prevents the door from opening while the drum is spinning. Never bypass it.

faq.q: Is F14 common?
faq.a: F14 is moderately common on older dryers. The lock mechanism wears over time from repeated use.

title: Waterproof System Activated
shortDescription: The leak detection system has detected water in the base of the dryer.
fullDescription: Error F15 indicates the float switch in the dryer's base pan has detected water. This is more common on condenser and heat pump models where condensate is collected.

causes.cause: Float switch stuck in activated position

fixSteps.title: Unplug and check base
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Tilt it back carefully and check the base pan for standing water. Dry with towels.

fixSteps.title: Check float switch
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the float switch moves freely. It may be stuck from a previous event.

fixSteps.title: Identify the leak
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect all internal water paths: condensate hoses, pump connections, container seat, and heat exchanger.

fixSteps.title: Repair the leak
fixSteps.instruction: Tighten or replace the leaking component. Ensure all connections are secure and leak-free.

whenToCallPro: If the leak source cannot be found, a technician should inspect all water-carrying components.
videoSearchQuery: Miele dryer F15 waterproof leak detection error fix

faq.q: Can a heat pump dryer leak water?
faq.a: Yes. Heat pump and condenser dryers extract moisture from clothes and collect it as water. Leaks can occur in the condensate path.

faq.q: How do I prevent F15?
faq.a: Regularly check and clean the condensate path. Ensure the water container is properly seated. Check hose connections periodically.

faq.q: Is F15 an emergency?
faq.a: While not as urgent as a washing machine leak, you should turn off the dryer and dry the base to prevent water damage to flooring.
